Mixbin Constants

The following sections contain the constants used by DirectSound that are associated with mixbins:

  • 5-Channel Support and HRTF
  • Mixbin Values
  • Mixbin Volume Pair Combinations

5-Channel Support and HRTF

When the DSMIXBIN_FRONT_CENTER flag is specified in the mixbin array for a 3D buffer or stream, audio will pan five ways with both full HRTF and light HRTF. DSMIXBIN_FRONT_CENTER should be specified at index four (the fifth element), following the standard required 3D mixbins. If the center channel is included in the mixbin array, but not at index four, the center channel will sound the same as any other non 3D mixbin; it will have distance attenuation applied, but no occlusion/obstruction volume or filtering.

To use 5-channel 3D, you must include the mixbins in DSMIXBINVOLUMEPAIRS_REQUIRED_5CHANNEL_3D, followed by any additionally desired mixbins, including I3DL2. Due to a hardware constraint, the normal occlusion/obstruction filtering that is done on the reverb data will be missing from 5-channel 3D. The direct-path filtering will still be present and will be on all 5 channels.

Mixbin Values

The following list contains the mixbin constants that are used by DirectSound for Xbox to determine input and output mixbins:

DSMIXBIN_BACK_LEFT
Specifies a fixed mixbin destination that goes to the back left speaker.
DSMIXBIN_BACK_RIGHT
Specifies a fixed mixbin destination that goes to the back right speaker.
DSMIXBIN_FRONT_LEFT
Specifies a fixed mixbin destination that goes to the front left speaker.
DSMIXBIN_FRONT_RIGHT
Specifies a fixed mixbin destination that goes to the front right speaker.
DSMIXBIN_FRONT_CENTER
Specifies a fixed mixbin destination that goes to the center speaker.
DSMIXBIN_I3DL2
Specifies the fixed mixbin that contains the reverberant field path audio data for an Interactive 3-D Audio Rendering Guidelines Level 2 (I3DL2) reverberation.
DSMIXBIN_FXSEND_0
Specifies a user-defined effects send mixbin destination. DirectMusic uses this mixbin for audio that should be processed by the reverberation effect.
DSMIXBIN_FXSEND_1
Specifies a user-defined effects send mixbin destination. DirectMusic uses this mixbin for audio that should be processed by the chorus effect.
DSMIXBIN_FXSEND_2
Specifies a user-defined effects send mixbin destination.
DSMIXBIN_FXSEND_3
Specifies a user-defined effects send mixbin destination.
DSMIXBIN_FXSEND_4
Specifies a user-defined effects send mixbin destination.
DSMIXBIN_FXSEND_5
Specifies a user-defined effects send mixbin destination.
DSMIXBIN_FXSEND_6
Specifies a user-defined effects send mixbin destination.
DSMIXBIN_FXSEND_7
Specifies a user-defined effects send mixbin destination.
DSMIXBIN_FXSEND_8
Specifies a user-defined effects send mixbin destination.
DSMIXBIN_FXSEND_9
Specifies a user-defined effects send mixbin destination.
DSMIXBIN_FXSEND_10
Specifies a user-defined effects send mixbin destination.
DSMIXBIN_FXSEND_11
Specifies a user-defined effects send mixbin destination.
DSMIXBIN_FXSEND_12
Specifies a user-defined effects send mixbin destination.
DSMIXBIN_FXSEND_13
Specifies a user-defined effects send mixbin destination.
DSMIXBIN_FXSEND_14
Specifies a user-defined effects send mixbin destination.
DSMIXBIN_FXSEND_15
Specifies a user-defined effects send mixbin destination.
DSMIXBIN_FXSEND_16
Specifies a user-defined effects send mixbin destination.
DSMIXBIN_FXSEND_17
Specifies a user-defined effects send mixbin destination.
DSMIXBIN_FXSEND_18
Specifies a user-defined effects send mixbin destination.
DSMIXBIN_FXSEND_19
Specifies a user-defined effects send mixbin destination.
DSMIXBIN_LOW_FREQUENCY
Specifies a fixed mixbin destination that goes to the low-frequency effects channel (not always the same as the subwoofer).
DSMIXBIN_SUBMIX
Specifies the mixbin that is used for submixing.
DSMIXBIN_XTLK_BACK_LEFT
Specifies the fixed mixbin destination that will be processed by the crosstalk cancellation algorithm and sent to the back left speaker. This mixbin is used internally by the HRTF 3D processing.
DSMIXBIN_XTLK_BACK_RIGHT
Specifies the fixed mixbin destination that will be processed by the crosstalk cancellation algorithm and sent to the back right speaker. This mixbin is used internally by the HRTF 3D processing.
DSMIXBIN_XTLK_FRONT_LEFT
Specifies the fixed mixbin destination that will be processed by the crosstalk cancellation algorithm and sent to the front left speaker. This mixbin is used internally by the HRTF 3D processing.
DSMIXBIN_XTLK_FRONT_RIGHT
Specifies the fixed mixbin destination that will be processed by the crosstalk cancellation algorithm and sent to the front right speaker. This mixbin is used internally by the HRTF 3D processing.

Mixbin Volume Pair Combinations

The following list contains the predefined combinations of mixbin volume pairs that are used by DirectSound for Xbox:

DSMIXBINVOLUMEPAIRS_DEFAULT_MONO
Specifies the default mono mixbins settings (DSMIXBIN_FRONT_LEFT and DSMIXBIN_FRONT_RIGHT). Counts as two mixbin volume pairs. The volume of each mixbin is set to zero.
DSMIXBINVOLUMEPAIRS_DEFAULT_STEREO
Specifies the default stereo mixbins settings (DSMIXBIN_FRONT_LEFT and DSMIXBIN_FRONT_RIGHT). Counts as two mixbin volume pairs. The volume of each mixbin is set to zero.
DSMIXBINVOLUMEPAIRS_DEFAULT_4CHANNEL
Specifies the default 4-channel audio mixbins settings (DSMIXBIN_FRONT_LEFT, DSMIXBIN_FRONT_RIGHT, DSMIXBIN_BACK_LEFT, and DSMIXBIN_BACK_RIGHT). Counts as four mixbin volume pairs. The volume of each mixbin is set to zero.
DSMIXBINVOLUMEPAIRS_DEFAULT_6CHANNEL
Specifies the default 6-channel audio mixbins settings (DSMIXBIN_FRONT_LEFT, DSMIXBIN_FRONT_RIGHT, DSMIXBIN_FRONT_CENTER, DSMIXBIN_LOW_FREQUENCY, DSMIXBIN_BACK_LEFT, and DSMIXBIN_BACK_RIGHT). Counts as six mixbin volume pairs. The volume of each mixbin is set to zero.
DSMIXBINVOLUMEPAIRS_REQUIRED_3D
Specifies the required 3D audio mixbins settings (DSMIXBIN_XTLK_FRONT_LEFT, DSMIXBIN_XTLK_BACK_LEFT, DSMIXBIN_XTLK_FRONT_RIGHT, and DSMIXBIN_XTLK_BACK_RIGHT). Counts as four mixbin volume pairs. The volume of each mixbin is set to zero. If these are the only mixbins included, the 3D algorithm will position sounds in the four corner (front/back right/left) speakers.
DSMIXBINVOLUMEPAIRS_DEFAULT_3D
Specifies the default 3D audio mixbins settings (DSMIXBIN_XTLK_FRONT_LEFT, DSMIXBIN_XTLK_BACK_LEFT, DSMIXBIN_XTLK_FRONT_RIGHT, DSMIXBIN_XTLK_BACK_RIGHT, and DSMIXBIN_I3DL2). Counts as five mixbin volume pairs. The volume of each mixbin is set to zero. If these are the only mixbins included, the 3D algorithm will position sounds in the four corner (front/back right/left) speakers and reverb is applied.
DSMIXBINVOLUMEPAIRS_REQUIRED_5CHANNEL_3D
Specifies the required five channel 3D audio mixbin settings (DSMIXBIN_3D_FRONT_LEFT, DSMIXBIN_3D_BACK_LEFT, DSMIXBIN_3D_FRONT_RIGHT, DSMIXBIN_3D_BACK_RIGHT, and DSMIXBIN_FRONT_CENTER). Counts as five mixbin volume pairs. The volume of each mixbin is set to zero. If these mixbins are used, this causes a 5-channel 3D algorithm to be used. Sounds will be positioned using the center speaker as well as the four corner speakers.
DSMIXBINVOLUMEPAIRS_DEFAULT_5CHANNEL_3D
Specifies the default five channel 3D audio mixbin settings (DSMIXBIN_3D_FRONT_LEFT, DSMIXBIN_3D_BACK_LEFT, DSMIXBIN_3D_FRONT_RIGHT, DSMIXBIN_3D_BACK_RIGHT, DSMIXBIN_FRONT_CENTER, and DSMIXBIN_I3DL2). Counts as six mixbin volume pairs. The volume of each mixbin is set to zero. If these mixbins are used, this causes a 5-channel 3D algorithm to be used. Sounds will be positioned using the center speaker as well as the four corner speakers and reverb is applied.
DSMIXBINVOLUMEPAIRS_DEFAULT_5CHANNEL_3D_PLUS_LFE
Specifies the default five channel 3D audio mixbin settings with LFE (DSMIXBIN_3D_FRONT_LEFT, DSMIXBIN_3D_BACK_LEFT, DSMIXBIN_3D_FRONT_RIGHT, DSMIXBIN_3D_BACK_RIGHT, DSMIXBIN_FRONT_CENTER, DSMIXBIN_I3DL2, and DSMIXBIN_LOW_FREQUENCY). Counts as seven mixbin volume pairs. The volume of each mixbin is set to zero. If these mixbins are used, this causes a 5-channel 3D algorithm to be used. Sounds will be positioned using the center speaker as well as the four corner speakers and reverb is applied. Sound is sent to the LFE speaker as well. (This setting is included for convenience; the LFE isn't specifically used by the HRTF algorithm.)
Unpublished work. © 2000 Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.
